title: Buttery Gooey Polish Duck
description: This delicious and indulgent dish is perfect for midnight cravings, featuring a blend of dry sherry, rich butter, and high-protein-friendly duck meat that melts in your mouth.

ingredients.name: Dry sherry
ingredients.notes: Sherry can be substituted with a dry white wine for non-alcoholic option

ingredients.name: Butter
ingredients.notes: For the crispy skin and gooey center

ingredients.name: All-purpose flour
ingredients.notes: For dredging the duck fillets in

instructions: Mix all-purpose flour with salt and black pepper. Dredge the duck fillets in the flour mixture.
Heat 2 tablespoons of butter in a skillet over medium heat. Sear the duck fillets on both sides until golden brown, about 3-4 minutes per side.
In another pan, sauté garlic, onion, and celery in remaining 2 tablespoons butter until tender.
Pour sherry into the pan with vegetables to deglaze it. Reduce the liquid by half while scraping the bottom of the pan for added flavor.
Place seared duck fillets in a baking dish. Pour vegetable mixture over them.
Drizzle remaining butter all over the duck, including the skin side. Cover with foil and bake at 350°F (175°C) for about 20 minutes.
Uncover and continue to cook for another 20-30 minutes or until internal temperature reaches 165°F (74°C).
Let it rest for a few minutes before slicing into wedges. Serve with your favorite sides.
Note: Cooking time can be adjusted based on duck size, adjust cooking heat as necessary.
